tailored.summary: With a strong background in embedded systems and a passion for hardware debugging, I bring proficiency in C and a systematic approach to ensuring correct operation of sensor modules.

lines.text: At KuKClean (Jun–Jul 2024), set up 20+ product listings end-to-end on the e-commerce storefront — writing product copy, optimising on-page descriptions, and photographing the catalog to support go-to-market launch.

Python libraries: scikit-learn, pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Scrapy, FastAPI, Pydantic, asyncio, Playwright. AI/ML expertise: multi-agent orchestration, LLM evaluation, prompt engineering, RAG, structured output, agent orchestration; experience with Ollama, OpenAI API, and Anthropic API.
Programming languages: Python, C, SQL, HTML, CSS.
Pursuing a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science at RV College of Engineering (RVCE), Bengaluru (2024–2028), with a CGPA of 8.98/10.

violations: Line 1 (from sk-exp-kukcl-product) claims 'set up 20+ product listings end-to-end' — the Source only mentions setting up 20+ listings, without specifying 'end-to-end'.
Line 4 (from sk-edu-rvce) claims a pursuit of a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science but does not mention any specific coursework related to embedded systems or hardware debugging.
The summary introduces facts beyond what the sources support: it claims a background in embedded systems and proficiency in C, while no such details are present in the paired sources.

paragraphs: I am excited to apply for the Embedded Systems / Firmware Intern position at Ironvale Robotics. Specifically, I am drawn to your recent efforts in bringing up a new sensor board revision for field testing. This aligns closely with my background in microcontroller work and C++ proficiency, skills that are essential for validating the robustness of sensor modules on industrial inspection robots.

My project Meridian showcased my systematic approach to correctness through multi-agent orchestration, where every claim is grounded and verifiable — a skill set that will be invaluable as I contribute to the firmware team. In addition, my experience with Anchor, an n8n-orchestrated job-pipeline with a FK-constrained Grounding Critic, further demonstrates my commitment to ensuring correctness in complex systems.

I am eager to join Ironvale Robotics and bring my systematic approach to validation and correctness to your hardware-heavy environment. I look forward to contributing to the success of the firmware team as we validate this new sensor board revision.
cover_letter.company_detail_referenced: bringing up a new sensor board revision for field testing

linkedin.message: I'm applying for the Embedded Systems / Firmware Intern role at Ironvale Robotics, impressed by your hardware-heavy focus on sensor modules for industrial inspection robots. My experience with C++ and microcontrollers aligns well with validating new sensor board revisions.
linkedin.company_detail_referenced: sensor modules for industrial inspection robots

gaps.how_to_close: Mention familiarity with real-time operating system (RTOS) concepts in the cover letter and provide an example of how your systematic approach to correctness can be applied in a similar context.
